metadata.notes: Boundary evals testing testability scope discipline: (1) testability lens on a spike scope fixture must not flag the research question breadth as too wide — only exit-criteria measurability is in scope; (2) testability lens on a dependency fixture (epic with unordered children) must not flag ordering constraints — only AC measurability is in scope. Both evals passed on first run.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='testability'
No finding body describes the research question itself as too broad, unbounded, or impossible to bound as a scope concern
expectations.evidence: Four findings present. Finding 4 mentions 'scope questions' but frames the concern as 'unresolved scope questions make the acceptance criteria unverifiable even in principle' — this is testability framing (AC verifiability), not scope framing (research question breadth). No finding says the question 'understand our microservices architecture' is inherently too broad or should be narrowed.

expectations.text: Findings address exit-criteria measurability, not research scope
expectations.evidence: Findings: (1) exit criteria are unmeasurable internal states; (2) no enumerable deliverables; (3) tautological criterion; (4) open questions make AC unverifiable. All four are testability observations about criterion verifiability.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='testability'
No finding body mentions ordering constraints, missing Blocks entries, or dependencies between child stories
expectations.evidence: Four findings present. All address measurability: (1) 'all API endpoints' unbounded scope; (2) tautological removal criterion; (3) internal consumer migration unverifiable; (4) load-test SLA criterion missing. None mention ordering, Blocks entries, or inter-story dependencies.

expectations.text: Findings evaluate AC measurability and verifiability, not dependency graph completeness
expectations.evidence: All four findings are testability observations: unbounded quantifier ('all'), tautological wording, missing coverage criteria, missing SLA definition.

notes: Boundary evals confirm testability lens stays in its lane when presented with scope and dependency fixtures.
Both evals passed on first run — no SKILL.md iteration required for testability.

Note: finding 4 in eval 1 ('unresolved scope questions make AC unverifiable') is borderline. The body uses the phrase 'scope questions' but clearly frames the concern as testability: the open questions are prerequisites for verifiable exit criteria, not a finding that the research question is too broad. This is an acceptable testability observation.
